name: Traditional Apple Butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Core and chop apples (do not peel).
2. Combine apples and cider in a slow-cooking pot.
3. Cover and cook on low for 10-12 hours or until apples are mushy.
4. Puree the mixture using a food mill or sieve.
5. Return the pureed mixture to the pot; add sugar, cinnamon, and cloves.
6. Cover and cook on low for one hour.
The apple butter will keep several weeks in the refrigerator. Alternatively, it can be poured into hot sterilized jars and sealed, or into freezer containers and frozen. This recipe makes approximately 8 cups.
